title: Sphaeralcea orbiculata
taxonomy: Sphaeralcea orbiculata is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is commonly known as the goldenrod or desert marigold, and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The species is a perennial herb that grows to be around 2-6 inches tall, with leaves that are orbicular in shape and flowers that are typically yellow in color. Sphaeralcea orbiculata is classified in the genus Sphaeralcea, which is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as the desert marigolds. The species is also classified in the subfamily Malvoideae and the order Malpighiales.

intro: Sphaeralcea orbiculata, also known as desert marigold, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and alleviate digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Sphaeralcea orbiculata include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, as well as sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
